_notes: Method-B v2: TurnRDv2 (bidirectional encoder, identifiable Σα·v R-loss, progress-prior init, no CLS bottleneck). Apples-to-apples vs method_b_lean (5×40 eps, K=4, real WebShop) — only the turnrd block differs. Architecture: 2 layers × 128 hidden (smaller than v1's 6×384 because v2's per-turn heads don't need to push through a CLS bottleneck). Loss mix: loss_v2_pred + 0.1·loss_v2_rank + 0.01·loss_v2_progress_prior; lambda_value=0 until per-turn sibling-CF target lands. Cache paths redirected to /vol/cache/method_b_v2/ so v1 ckpts don't get loaded by mistake. Goal: confirm whether v2's sharper α concentration (validated synthetically) translates into a measurable RL win over method_b_lean (R≈0.10, success=0.265 baseline).
